---
title: "stressPLS methodology"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{stressPLS methodology}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Overview

`stressPLS` is designed to evaluate whether formative higher-order construct
results in PLS-SEM remain stable under reasonable perturbations.

The framework separates five responsibilities:

1. Model specification.
2. Perturbation generation.
3. Model estimation.
4. Robustness scoring.
5. Reporting and visualization.

This initial scaffold implements the package structure, input validation,
perturbation grids, S3 result classes, summaries, plotting, and reporting
placeholders. It does not implement the full methodology or a PLS-SEM backend.

## Perturbation families

The planned stress-test families include indicator deletion, indicator swapping,
construct reweighting, bootstrap sensitivity, and alternative higher-order
construct specifications. Current functions generate explicit scenario grids
that backends can consume later.
